title: DeepSlide: From Artifacts to Presentation Delivery
optimized_headline: DeepSlide: Transforming Artifacts into Engaging Presentation Tools

raw_body:
arXiv:2605.15202v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Presentations are a primary medium for scholarly communication, yet most AI slide generators optimize the artifact (a visually plausible deck) while under-optimizing the delivery process (pacing, narrative, and presentation preparation). We present DeepSlide, a human-in-the-loop multi-agent system that supports preparing the full presentation process, from requirement elicitation and time-budgeted narrative planning, to evidence-grounded slide--script generation, attention augmentation, and rehearsal support. DeepSlide integrates (i) a controllable logical-chain planner with per-node time budgets, (ii) a lightweight content-tree retriever for grounding, (iii) Markov-style sequential rendering with style inheritance, and (iv) sandboxed execution with minimal repair to ensure renderability. We further introduce a dual-scoreboard benchmark that cleanly separates static artifact quality from dynamic delivery excellence. Across 20 domains and diverse audience profiles, DeepSlide matches strong baselines on artifact quality while consistently achieving larger gains on delivery metrics, improving narrative flow, pacing precision, and slide--script synergy with clearer attention guidance.
speedrun: DeepSlide is a new AI system designed to enhance presentations by focusing not just on creating visually appealing slides but also on improving delivery. It operates through a multi-agent approach, helping users with everything from planning to rehearsal. Notably, it has shown improvements in narrative flow and pacing across 20 different domains. This matters now as effective communication is crucial in scholarly environments, where clarity can influence understanding and engagement.
why_it_matters: Researchers and presenters could benefit immediately from improved narrative flow and pacing, enhancing their communication effectiveness.
This development reflects a broader trend in AI toward not just creating content but also optimizing the way that content is delivered, making presentations more impactful.

lenses.eli12: DeepSlide is like having a personal coach for your presentations. It helps you not only create slides but also practice how to present them effectively. This is important for everyone because better presentations can lead to clearer ideas and more engaging discussions in any setting.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, DeepSlide addresses a key user need for effective communication tools. By improving delivery metrics, it could enhance user engagement and retention. This means that investing in such technology could lead to more successful presentations and better audience responses.
lenses.engineer: DeepSlide employs a multi-agent system that integrates a logical-chain planner and a lightweight content retriever. It uses Markov-style rendering for dynamic slide generation and introduces a dual-scoreboard benchmark to evaluate both static and dynamic aspects of presentations. This approach allows for significant improvements in delivery metrics, ensuring that the slides and scripts work well together.
